title: CVSS Distribution
spl:
index=security tag=vulnerability status=open
| stats count by severity
| sort severity
description: Histogram of `severity` / CVSS for backlog risk profile.
value: Histogram of `severity` / CVSS for backlog risk profile.
implementation: Filter internet-exposed assets via lookup for criticality weighting.
cimSpl: | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities where Vulnerabilities.status="open" by Vulnerabilities.severity
knownFalsePositives: Vulnerability aging and risk noise when CMDB sync lags, accepted risks are not in the report, or scan windows miss maintenance clusters; de-duplicate by asset and exception.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Vulnerabilities CIM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `severity` field (critical/high/medium/low) or numeric `cvss`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Filter internet-exposed assets via lookup for criticality weighting.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=vulnerability status=open
| stats count by severity
| sort severity
```

Understanding this SPL

**CVSS Distribution** — Histogram of `severity` / CVSS for backlog risk profile.

Documented **Data sources**: `severity` field (critical/high/medium/low) or numeric `cvss`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Vulnerabilities CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities where Vulnerabilities.status="open" by Vulnerabilities.severity
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**CVSS Distribution** — Histogram of `severity` / CVSS for backlog risk profile.

Documented **Data sources**: `severity` field (critical/high/medium/low) or numeric `cvss`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Vulnerabilities CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (severity counts), Pie chart.
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "CVSS Distribution" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
